data.body:
User feedback from /taxonomize review (2026-04-16): The CLI currently has both 'kno skill' (prints the skill prompt for a knot's next action state) and 'kno skills' (install/update managed skill bundles for Claude/Codex/OpenCode). The two nouns are easily confused. Rename 'skill' to 'prompt' so the commands read:

- kno prompt <knot-id>  -> print the action-state prompt for a knot (what 'kno skill' does today)
- kno skills install/uninstall/update -> unchanged (managed skills installation into external tools)

Scope of change:
- src/cli.rs: rename Commands::Skill variant and its args struct; keep an alias 'skill' for backward compat (one release cycle).
- src/run_commands.rs: rename run_skill -> run_prompt (and update dispatch).
- src/cli_skills.rs: review whether it should split into cli_prompt.rs + cli_skills.rs.
- docs/ and skills/*.md that describe the command.
- TAXONOMY.md: remove the 'skill vs skills' ambiguity flag.

Acceptance: 'kno prompt <id>' prints the prompt; 'kno skill <id>' is either removed or prints a deprecation notice. 'kno skills install' continues to work unchanged.
data.title: Rename CLI command 'skill' to 'prompt'
